channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: The 20-year-old who stole the show on Scotland's World Cup return
item.description: Ben Gannon-Doak wasn't born when Scotland last played at the men's World Cup. But the 20-year-old was at the heart of their long-awaited return and their first win at the tournament in 36 years as he stole the show against Haiti.

item.title: BBC Sport pundits choose England World Cup XIs - who would you pick?
item.description: Thomas Tuchel will pick his first England World Cup team on Wednesday, when the Three Lions take on Croatia. BBC Sport pundits choose their starting XIs - who would you pick?

item.title: Has Vinicius Jr brilliance just papered over the Brazilian cracks?
item.description: Vinicius Jr scores a wonder goal to rescue his side but Brazil's performance against Morocco raises more questions than answers.

item.title: Refugee who quit Bayern to create Aussie World Cup dream
item.description: Nestory Irankunda was born as a refugee in Tanzania before becoming Australian record breaker with goal in victory over Turkey.

item.title: Why Haiti v Scotland was antidote to the ills of world football
item.description: It might have been nervy. It might not have been pretty. But Scotland are here. And Scotland have won. Tom English reports from Boston.

item.title: McGinn fires Scotland to first World Cup win for 36 years
item.description: John McGinn's goal was enough as Scotland beat Haiti 1-0 at the Boston Stadium to record their first win at a World Cup for 36 years.

item.title: Clinical Australia upset Turkey in World Cup opener
item.description: Watch highlights as Australia beat Turkey in their opening match, with 20-year-old Nestory Irankunda becoming the Socceroos's youngest ever World Cup goalscorer.

item.title: Fifa to pay Somali referee Artan full World Cup fee
item.description: Somali referee Omar Artan, who was denied entry to the United States to officiate at the World Cup, will still receive his tournament fee from Fifa in full.

item.title: Why can't World Cup games kick off on time?
item.description: A delay to Scotland's Group C tie with Haiti in Massachusetts continued a noticeable trend of tardiness at the 2026 tournament, with none of the first eight matches kicking off on time.

item.title: Smaller than Isle of Man & huge Dutch influence: Curacao making history
item.description: Curacao has possibly been most famous for its alcoholic drink of the same name - but now the nation is ready to announce itself at the World Cup.

item.title: Could Japan be the World Cup's dark horses?
item.description: Japan could be one of the surprise packages of the 2026 World Cup, with growing belief they are ready to go further than ever before.

item.title: Fifa World Cup 2026: What you need to know about Germany
item.description: Get the lowdown on every team at the 2026 World Cup. Here we examine European powerhouses Germany.

item.title: What does 1-0 win mean for Scotland's chances of qualification?
item.description: With eight third-place finishers advancing to the last 32, might three points be enough for Scotland to make history?

item.title: Was this offside? Technology fault leaves questions over VAR images
item.description: Fifa have blamed a technical fault for the delay in releasing the VAR images after a potential - and controversial - offside call during Qatar's World Cup draw with Switzerland.

item.title: Who am I? Guess World Cup star No 7
item.description: Will you get today's player in as few attempts as possible?

item.title: Shirts, stuffed lions & Lego - the stolen England items worth $18,000
item.description: Two men are charged for stealing $18,000 worth of property from England at their Kansas City training base.

item.title: McGinn 'beaming with pride' - and hopes kids around Scotland are too
item.description: Scotland's John McGinn says his "scuffed" goal which downed Haiti in their World Cup opener leaves him "beaming with pride".

item.title: England's first World Cup training session: What did we learn?
item.description: England have had their first training session in Kansas City - what did we learn?

item.title: A new way to experience the World Cup - only on BBC Sport
item.description: Ever wanted to see a World Cup match from every angle? How about a full-pitch tactical view? Now you can on the BBC.

item.title: Catch up on the World Cup without any spoilers
item.description: It is a dilemma plenty of fans in the UK will have - and we are giving you the chance to follow the World Cup without spoilers.

item.title: Play BBC Sport's new World Cup predictor game
item.description: Play BBC Sport's new World Cup predictor game to be in with the chance of winning a signed football.

item.title: The World Cup is bigger than ever - and BBC Sport has an app to match
item.description: BBC Sport are launching a new, improved app experience for the Fifa World Cup 2026

item.title: Spectacular Vinicius Jr goal earns Brazil draw against Morocco
item.description: Vinicius Jr scores a spectacular solo goal to earn a draw for five-time winners Brazil against Morocco at MetLife Stadium in New Jersey.

item.title: McGinn gives Scotland the lead against Haiti
item.description: John McGinn puts Scotland 1-0 up against Haiti in their Group C opener at the Boston Stadium.

item.title: 'Just listen to this!' - Scotland are back at the World Cup after 28 years
item.description: Scotland's players and fans sing Flower of Scotland at a World Cup for the first time in 28 years before their match against Haiti at the Boston Stadium.

item.title: Saibari's 'clinical' finish gives Morocco lead against Brazil
item.description: Morocco's Ismael Saibari fires his side into the lead against Brazil with a clinical finish in the 21st minute of their Group C match at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: 'A must win game and we won' - Clarke
item.description: Scotland manager Steve Clarke reacts to his side's 1-0 victory over Haiti in their Group C opener at the Boston Stadium.

item.title: Arsenal and Liverpool chase Barcola - Sunday's gossip
item.description: Arsenal and Liverpool are among Bradley Barcola's suitors, Brighton reject Coventry's Carl Rushworth bid, Bayern Munich might beat Barcelona to Marcus Rashford, plus more.

item.title: Leicester close in on Martin appointment
item.description: Russell Martin, who led Southampton into the Premier League in 2024, has been out of work since being sacked by Rangers in October.

item.title: Fulham in advanced talks to appoint Arbeloa as Silva's replacement
item.description: Fulham are in advanced talks to appoint Alvaro Arbeloa as a replacement for outgoing manager Marco Silva.

item.title: Why Pulisic is ready to take USA to next level - Giroud
item.description: France's World Cup winner Olivier Giroud explains why his friend and former Chelsea and AC Milan team-mate Christian Pulisic holds the key to USA hopes at this summer's tournament.

item.title: Vinicius Jr scores 'lightning bolt' equaliser for Brazil
item.description: Brazil's Vinicius Jr fires home a "lightning bolt" of a strike to level up the scores in against Morocco in their Group C match in the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Switzerland stunned by late Qatar equaliser
item.description: Qatar's Boualem Khoukhi scores a stoppage-time equaliser to earn his country their first ever World Cup point against Switzerland in San Francisco.

item.title: Khoukhi heads home late equaliser for Qatar against Switzerland
item.description: Qatar's Boualem Khoukhi heads home an equaliser in added on time as his side earn their first ever World Cup point against Switzerland in San Francisco.

item.title: Was there an offside in build up to Switzerland penalty?
item.description: Breel Embolo puts Switzerland 1-0 up against Qatar from the penalty spot, after the Swiss were awarded a penalty with Remo Freuler appearing to be offside before he was fouled by Qatar goalkeeper Mahmoud Abunada.

item.title: Balogun stars in impressive USA win over Paraguay
item.description: Folarin Balogun scores twice as the USA begin their World Cup campaign with a convincing 4-1 victory over Paraguay at the Los Angeles Stadium.

item.title: Katy Perry, Tyla & Future among performers at USA's opening ceremony
item.description: Katy Perry, Tyla and Future are among the performers at USA's star-studded World Cup opening ceremony at SoFi Stadium Stadium.

item.title: Rooney and Cann strongly disagree on Bosnia goalkeeper incident
item.description: Former World Cup final assistant referee Darren Cann and Wayne Rooney disagree over an incident involving Bosnia-Herzegovina goalkeeper Nikola Vasilj, with Rooney arguing the goalkeeper should have been sent off for his challenge on Canada's Tani Oluwaseyi.

item.title: Amorim a leading contender for AC Milan job
item.description: Former Manchester United head coach Ruben Amorim emerges as a leading contender to replace Massimiliano Allegri at AC Milan.

item.title: Ghana government wants review of Partey Canada ban
item.description: Ghana midfielder Thomas Partey is banned from entering Canada for their opening game of the World Cup.

item.title: Injured referee Oliver to miss World Cup match
item.description: English referee Michael Oliver is ruled out of the group match between Ivory Coast and Ecuador.

item.title: New rules abound - was VAR diving decision wrong despite feeling right?
item.description: Some new rules and tweaks to a few old ones have been introduced for the 2026 World Cup. But are the changes leading to confusion?

item.title: Iran win four staff visa appeals but 11 banned
item.description: Four members of Iran's World Cup delegation win appeals against having their visa applications rejected but 11 staff remain banned from travelling to the United States.

item.title: USA start World Cup in style - but will they finally join the elite?
item.description: If there were any doubts the USA were ready to live up to the pressure of being a World Cup host, they were dispelled in a 4-1 win over Paraguay.

item.title: Arsenal consider move for Greek forward Tzolis
item.description: Arsenal have been offered Club Brugge forward Christos Tzolis and are considering accelerating a move for the Greece international.

item.title: Scotland boss Clarke by those who know him best
item.description: As Steve Clarke prepares to lead Scotland at their first men's World Cup since 1998, those who know him well reveal what makes him successful.

item.title: Bellingham set to start, but no Guehi - England's expected team
item.description: Jude Bellingham is expected to start England's opening game of the 2026 World Cup, as Marc Guehi could start on the bench.

item.title: West Ham shareholders address Sullivan allegations and agree ownership deal
item.description: Co-chairs Daniel Kretinsky and Vanessa Gold have spoken out on the allegations against David Sullivan as the Czech billionaire becomes the club's largest shareholder.

item.title: Six Lionesses made MBEs in King's Birthday Honours
item.description: Kevin Sinfield has received a knighthood in the King's Birthday Honours while six of England's Euro 2025-winning squad have been made MBEs.

item.title: Premier League target hair pulling and set-piece grappling in rules review
item.description: Premier League referees have been told to change the way they judge hair pulling after three players were sent off last season.

item.title: When do England play and how can you follow it?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything looks at England's next World Cup fixture and how you can follow along via the BBC.

item.title: Who will win the World Cup? BBC pundits make their predictions
item.description: Who will win, who might spring a surprise and how will England and Scotland do? BBC pundits predict what will happen at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Gerrard? McInnes? What next for Rangers if Rohl leaves?
item.description: With Rangers boss Danny Rohl reportedly in talks with RB Salzburg, where might the Ibrox club turn next?

item.title: Any early shocks? Sutton's World Cup 2026 score predictions - opening group games
item.description: BBC Sport's football expert Chris Sutton gives his predictions for the scores of the first round of group games at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Drop deep, cash in - why Kane can be England's free-scoring false nine
item.description: How has Harry Kane managed to enjoy the best goalscoring season of his career at Bayern Munich when dropping deep so often? Thomas Tuchel knows - and England might now feel the benefit.

item.title: 'Mazadona', 'Einstein', 'The Ant' - 20 new players to watch at the 2026 World Cup
item.description: BBC Sport TV and radio commentators pick the players they think could be the breakout stars of the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: The wild hip-hop parties that started Tuchel's journey to England boss
item.description: Thomas Tuchel is bidding to become the first foreign manager to win the World Cup. BBC Sport charts the start of his journey to becoming England boss.

item.title: All done deals in June 2026
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: What are the deadlines for World Cup squads?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains the deadlines by which teams must name their final squads for the 2026 World Cup

item.title: How much prize money have Premier League teams earned in Europe?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains how much Arsenal, Aston Villa and Crystal Palace have earned in Europe this season

item.title: Which club received most red cards in a Premier League season?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything looks at the teams who have received the most red cards in a Premier League season.

item.title: Who won the Premier League golden boot?
item.description: BBC's Ask Me Anything team explains who has won the Premier League golden boot and other individual awards.

item.title: Who are the oldest players to feature at a World Cup?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at who the oldest players are in the history of the World Cup

item.title: Who has qualified for next season's Champions League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains which teams have already qualified for the 2026-27 edition of the Champions League.

item.title: Who has the most Premier League assists in a season?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team look at who has provided the most assists in the history of the Premier League.

item.title: Rangers agree deal for Hearts boss McInnes - gossip
item.description: Several reports say head coach Derek McInnes is on the verge of completing a switch from Tynecastle to Ibrox...

item.title: Weir's title regret as she confirms Real Madrid exit
item.description: Scotland midfielder Caroline Weir has confirmed her exit from Real Madrid after four years with the La Liga runners-up without giving a hint of her next destination.

item.title: How has world changed since Scotland were last at men's finals?
item.description: When Scotland last reached a men's World Cup, the Scottish parliament didn't exist, Google wasn't a thing and a pint of lager was roughly £1.90.

item.title: Free agent Wales players put themselves in shop window
item.description: Rhian Wilkinson says her free-agent internationals gave potential suitors a glimpse of their qualities by helping Wales finish top of their Women's World Cup qualifying group.

item.title: Man City sign England's Mead on free transfer
item.description: Manchester City have confirmed the signing of England forward Beth Mead on a free transfer from Arsenal.

item.title: Man City agree deal to sign England's Charles
item.description: Manchester City have agreed a deal to sign England defender Niamh Charles from Chelsea.

item.title: London Stadium and West Ham women's team not aware of Sullivan contact ban
item.description: The owner of West Ham United's home has raised concerns it was not told about safeguarding restrictions.

item.title: National League clubs urge FA to end proposals over academy teams
item.description: Fifty two Women's National League clubs sign a letter sent to the Football Association opposing proposals to bring top tier academy teams into their league.

item.title: How Cornish miners brought football to Mexico
item.description: As Mexico prepares to become the first country to host or co-host the World Cup three times, BBC Sport looks at its Cornish footballing roots.

item.title: The summer a golden generation lost its shine
item.description: The summer of 2006 was meant to be England's moment. A world-class squad, celebrity partners and confident fans - the hype was huge and the fall was hard.

item.title: How Toone is navigating grief through football
item.description: England and Manchester United midfielder Ella Toone opens up about grief, football and preparing for her wedding without her dad in a new BBC documentary.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.
